message:
Replace recursive CTE with Rust-side BFS for descendant issue queries

The WITH RECURSIVE subquery used for --parent --recursive filtering is
not supported by fsqlite in subquery context. This caused silent failures
when filtering issues by parent with the recursive flag.

The fix replaces the SQL-side recursive CTE with a Rust-side BFS
traversal (collect_descendant_ids) that iteratively queries the
dependencies table and collects all transitive children. The collected
IDs are then passed as a flat IN(...) clause with positional parameters.

Additional fixes:
- The parent_id parameter push was previously outside the if/else
  branches, meaning it was bound even for recursive queries that used
  a CTE with its own parameter. Moved the push into the non-recursive
  branch where it belongs.
- The get-dependents query now also selects i.created_at to match the
  IssueWithDependencyMetadata struct expectations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
